includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database

contaminant.name: Organic Biofilm Deposits
contaminant.description: Biological stains, they arise from algae, bacteria, and organic waste that accumulate and form adherent films on surfaces after prolonged exposure to moisture. This contamination, it stands out from mineral deposits by its bio-adhesive nature and irregular layering, thus clings variably—tightly on porous stones like limestone and loosely on smooth metals like stainless steel. Removal challenges stem from deep penetration and regeneration risks, yet laser cleaning succeeds through targeted ablation that vaporizes organics cleanly and preserves substrate integrity.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Carbon Dioxide, Formaldehyde - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Formaldehyde, Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
